steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage contrasts two recruitment methods: snowball sampling (through social networks) produced much higher retention than recruitment by strangers. The question asks what best explains why social-network recruitment led to higher retention.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'being recruited by someone you know creates an obligation to persist': directly explains the retention difference — a social bond creates pressure to continue participating. ✓
  • Choice B — 'snowball sampling reaches a different population': addresses who is recruited, not why those recruits stayed longer. ✗
  • Choice C — 'stranger-recruited participants were less motivated': the passage does not compare motivation levels; this goes beyond what the data support. ✗
  • Choice D — 'social connection is unimportant to study design': contradicts the passage, which treats social connection as the key variable. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
